caption: Slice 2 of the nested atomic order queue (#826): a multi-unit drag-line form-up now builds a shared Order.Type.FORM_UP tag for the group instead of N unrelated standalone orders -- an internal representation change only, with NO intended difference in what a form-up looks like. Watch three regiments (Spearmen 140, Infantry 120, Archers 90) get box-selected, then a single right-drag sends each on its OWN march toward a distinct slice of the flank line -- exactly as before this slice: same per-unit destinations, same timing, still three separately visible march paths, not a single merged movement. State-dump verified across the full clip (ticks 8/60/140/180/220/260/300). All three units finish reshaping frontage by tick 60 (Spearmen 16->20, Infantry 15->18, Archers already at 13). After that they march on very different schedules: Archers is at full speed by tick 140 and holds it through tick 300, Infantry stays IDLE until tick 300 then just starts accelerating, and Spearmen never leaves its post-reform IDLE hold within the clip. This staggered reform-then-hold timing pre-dates the slice and is untouched by its order-tree change. All ten units still show engaged=false at tick 300, so none are in contact yet; Archers is the closest team-0 unit to the enemy line by then (about 166 units from its counterpart, versus 240-320 for the other four), consistent with the clip's 300-tick cutoff landing before any unit reaches contact. Covered by new GUT tests asserting the shared FORM_UP parent/children wiring and a regression test proving the tree's cascade still stops correctly for a grouped order (Unit._advance_order_tree's hardened stop condition, a forward-looking guard, not a fix for a currently-reachable bug).
